generation_note: Hand-authored — generate-seed-evals.js emits sibling-skill-target prompts unsuitable for brain skills

evals.prompt: Learn from this — I had to correct you three times today about editing SKILL.md.baseline files. Please capture that so it doesn't happen again.

workflow_steps.step: Draft candidate learning with How to apply
Present draft for confirmation via AskUserQuestion

judgment: Drafts an anti-pattern (repeated mistake, 'never this') with a behavioral How-to-apply rule, not a blame note
Presents the draft for per-item confirmation before writing anything
Runs brain-capture.js with --type lesson (title prefixed anti-pattern:) only after confirmation, then brain-compile

evals.prompt: Don't do that again — you kept re-running the full test suite instead of just the changed package's tests. Can we make sure that sticks for next time?

workflow_steps.step: Draft candidate learning with How to apply

judgment: Recognizes 'don't do that again' as the aprende trigger despite no literal 'learn from this' phrasing
Produces the same confirm-then-write outcome as the direct-trigger scenario

evals.prompt: /aprende — I corrected your approach to the retry logic twice this session. Draft the lesson, but I want final say before anything gets written.

workflow_steps.step: Present draft and wait for explicit confirm/edit/reject
Do not run brain-capture.js until confirmation received

judgment: Triggers on the explicit /aprende slash pattern and drafts the lesson
Correctly treats 'final say' as reinforcing the skill's own hard rule — presents the draft and stops short of writing until the user confirms, edits, or rejects it
If the user rejects the draft in this turn, no brain-capture.js call happens and no trace is left, per 'zero writes before confirmation'

evals.prompt: What's the actual difference between a lesson and an anti-pattern in the brain system? I keep mixing up when to use which.

judgment: Correctly reads this as a conceptual/explanatory question, not a request to capture a specific correction
Answers conversationally (lesson = do this, anti-pattern = never this) without drafting or writing anything

evals.prompt: This is the second time this session I've had to walk you back from force-pushing over a teammate's branch — we need to make sure this behavior is corrected going forward, not just fixed for now.

workflow_steps.step: Draft candidate anti-pattern with How to apply

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('second time... walked you back', 'corrected going forward') identifies a repeated-error learning signal without the literal words 'learn' or 'aprende'
Classifies as anti-pattern (never force-push over a teammate's branch) rather than a routine lesson

evals.prompt: Just log this decision that we chose Postgres over SQLite for the new service — nothing dramatic, just want it on record.

judgment: Adversarial probe: a routine decision with no correction/mistake signal — this is brain-capture's job (type=decision), not capture-learning's
Must NOT invoke capture-learning's draft-then-confirm workflow — there was no error or reversal to learn from

evals.prompt: /aprende — I had to correct you twice today for hardcoding a skill count instead of discovering skills dynamically via `find skills/ -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d`. Capture that as an anti-pattern.

workflow_steps.step: Draft candidate anti-pattern with How to apply

judgment: Output references the project's actual CLAUDE.md rule (discover skills dynamically, never hardcode a skill count)
Uses the anti-pattern: title prefix so brain-compile classifies it correctly
Integrates with the project's Node.js stack (script invocation only, no build step) and writes nothing before confirmation

evals.prompt: During skill-refine on skill-scout, I had to stop you from committing evals/ artifacts — remember, evals/ is gitignored and should never be committed. Learn from this so it doesn't happen again in future refine loops.

workflow_steps.step: Draft candidate anti-pattern with How to apply

judgment: Activates correctly within the skill-refine loop context in the project's pipeline, without duplicating skill-refine's own iteration log
Does not attempt to log this as a routine decision (brain-capture) — treats it as a correction worth an anti-pattern entry, confirmed before write

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We agreed the mistake worth capturing is that you deployed via ecc-agentshield --opus automatically without asking, which the project rules forbid — it makes live API calls. We already drafted the anti-pattern and I approved it. Let's continue — go ahead and write it now.

workflow_steps.step: Run brain-capture.js using the already-confirmed draft

judgment: Triggers correctly despite continuation/resumption framing
Does not re-draft or re-ask for confirmation already given earlier in the session — proceeds straight to the write since approval was already established
Writes the anti-pattern (title prefixed anti-pattern:) referencing the --opus auto-call rule and reports the file written
